excerpt: The Prophet's renunciation of the world (zuhd) and his rebuke of worldly attachments is a recurring theme in early Ismaili jurisprudence, where worldly wealth is figured as a seductress to be dismissed.
source_location_hint: Kitab al-Iman, sections on zuhd and the Prophet's character

excerpt: The taʾwīl of the Qurʾānic narrative of ʿĪsā reads the figure of the Messiah as a typological cipher for the Imām of the cycle, so that the polemic of the Prophet's contemporaries against the 'son of Mary' is understood as their polemic against the wilāya of ʿAlī.
source_location_hint: Sections on the prophetic cycles and the taʾwīl of ʿĪsā

excerpt: The community of the Prophet split, in respect of the wilāya of ʿAlī, into those who exceeded all bounds by deifying him, those who denied him his right, and those who recognised him in his true station as waṣī and walī of the believers.
source_location_hint: Discourses on the wilāya of the Commander of the Faithful
